zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir Robert Lockhart, Baron of Lee Castle於1591年出生在英國蘇格蘭藍納可爾可萊時，他的父親Lord, Alan Lockhart VI of Ceghorn39歲，母親Lady, Elizabeth, M. Lockhart of Cleghorn31歲。他於1617年與Dame, Anna Elphinstone在英國蘇格蘭結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他去世於1663年3月2日在他的家鄉享年72歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir Robert Lockhart, Baron of Lee Castle于1591年出生在英国苏格兰藍納可爾可萊时，他的父亲Lord, Alan Lockhart VI of Ceghorn39岁，母亲Lady, Elizabeth, M. Lockhart of Cleghorn31岁。他于1617年与Dame, Anna Elphinstone在英国苏格兰结婚。他们至少是3个儿子和1个女儿的父母。他去世于1663年3月2日在他的家乡享年72岁。

nameMeanings.name: Lockhart,
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish and English: nickname from Old French locart, a Normanno-Picard form of Old French lo(s)chart ‘one who squints, who is cross-eyed’, attested in 13th- and 14th-century Arras (Pas-de-Calais) as Louchard, Lochard, Locard and Locart. It is a derivative of Latin luscus ‘one-eyed’. In France Locard is mostly found in Normandy (especially Calvados) c. 1900, while Louchart is mainly concentrated in Pas-de-Calais at the same time.
Americanized form of German   Luckhardt  .

History: According to family tradition, Sir Simon Locard held the key to the locked silver casket in which Robert the Bruce's heart was taken to be buried in the Holy Land in 1329. This unverifiable story is no doubt related to the later spelling of the Scottish surname as Lockhart and to the addition of a heart and fetterlock to the family coat of arms (date unknown).

variants.name: Lockard
meanings: Scottish and English: variant of   Lockhart   1 and 2.

variants.name: Lockart
meanings: Scottish and English: variant of   Lockhart  .

variants.name: Lockheart
meanings: Scottish: variant of   Lockhart  .

variants.name: Lockert
meanings: German: variant of   Lockhart   3.

variants.name: Luckhardt
meanings: German: from the ancient Germanic female personal name Liutgard, a compound of liud ‘people, tribe’ + gard ‘protective enclosure, yard’. Liutgard was a popular name in medieval times; among others it was the name of the daughter of emperor Otto I and the wife of Charlemagne.

variants.name: Lochard
meanings: Scottish: variant of   Lockhart  .
French: from a derivative of Old French loche ‘loach’, later also ‘species of giant slug’, hence, in the latter case, a nickname for a slow man. This surname is also found in Haiti.

nameMeanings.name: Baron
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: French, English, Dutch, Polish, Czech, Romanian, and Croatian; Breton (also Le Baron): from Old French barun, baron, Middle English barun, baroun, Middle Dutch baroen, Polish, Czech, Romanian, and dialectal Croatian baron ‘baron’ (i.e. a nobleman ranking below an earl and above a knight), a title of nobility which was in England also awarded to certain freemen of the cities of London and York and of the Cinque Ports. As a surname it is unlikely to be a status name denoting a person of rank. Generally, it referred to service in a baronial household or was acquired as a nickname by a peasant who had ideas above his station or applied to a man showing characteristics of a nobleman. See also 3 and 4 below, compare Barron and Lebaron .
French: habitational name from (Le) Baron, the name of several places in various parts of France. Compare   Lebaron  .
German: status name for a freeman or baron, from barūn ‘imperial or church official’, a loanword in Middle High German from Old French (see 1 above).

variants.name: Barron
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Juan, Jesus, Manuel, Francisco, Carlos, Miguel, Raul, Pedro, Ruben, Alfredo, Luis.

Scottish: status name from Scots baron, which in the Highlands denoted a small proprietary landholder. In Angus it alluded specifically to a tenant of one of the small baronies attached to the Abbey of Coupar Angus. The surname was taken by Scottish migrants to Ulster. Compare Baron .
Scottish: shortened form of McBarron, from Scottish Gaelic Mac a' Bharain ‘son of the baron’. See Baron and 1 above.

variants.name: Boron
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Polish Krystyna, Edyta, Leszek, Mieczyslaw, Mieczyslawa, Wladyslaw.
Polish (Boroń): from a pet form of the personal names Borzysław or Borzymir, formed with the element bor ‘to fight’ (from Old Slavic boriti se).
French: habitational name from a place so called in Franche-Comté.

variants.name: Baro
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Mario, Manuel, Ana, Angel, Carlos, Cesar, Fernando, Jesus, Jose, Orlando, Ramon, Rosalia.  Italian Guido, Angelo, Carmelo, Federico, Lucio.
Catalan (Baró): status name from baró ‘free man, baron’ (see Baron 1), or a habitational name from a place so named.
Hungarian (Báró): status name from báró ‘baron’. In the form Baró, it could be from a pet form of the personal names Barabás (see Barabas ) or Bertalan.

variants.name: Barona
meanings: Spanish: from the feminine form of Barón ‘baron’ (see Baron ), applied as a nickname or as an occupational name for a member of the household of a baroness.
Italian: probably from the feminine form of   Barone  . The name is concentrated in Sondrio province (Lombardy).

variants.name: Barren
meanings: English: probably a variant of   Baron   or   Barran  .

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Young, Sung, Jung, Yong, Chang, Kwang, Sang, Dong, Hyun, Jae, Jin, Kyung, Jong, Chong, Chung, Byung, Jeong, Pyong, Myong, Hak, Jin Hwan, Sook, Yeong, Choon.
Korean: variant of   O  .
Japanese: variant of Ō (see O ).

variants.name: O
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Young, Bo Young, Chae, Chong Hwan, Dong Kun, Ho, Hwan, Hyun, Jong, Jung, Ki Soon, Kwang.  Korean Min, Chong, Chol, Jung Ho, Myong, Sa, Sung Kyun.

Korean (오; Chinese character: 吳): historically, there were two Chinese characters for the O surname, but by the late Koryŏ period (14th century AD ), one of them had disappeared. Some records indicate that there are as many as 210 different clans for the remaining character, but only sixteen can be positively documented. All sixteen of these clans originate from the same ancestor, O Ch’ŏm (吳 瞻), who migrated to Korea from China during the reign of Shilla's King Chijŭng (500–514 AD ). O is a common surname throughout Korea. Compare Oh .

nameMeanings.name: Lee
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Young, Sang, Jae, Jong, Jung, Sung, Yong, Kyung, Seung, Dong, Kwang, Myung.

English: topographic name for someone who lived near a meadow or a patch of arable land, Middle English lee, lea, from Old English lēa, dative case (used after a preposition) of lēah, which originally meant ‘wood or glade’.

English: habitational name from any of the many places in England named with Old English lēah ‘wood, glade’, including Lee in Buckinghamshire, Essex, Hampshire, Kent, and Shropshire, and Lea in Cheshire, Derbyshire, Herefordshire, Lancashire, Lincolnshire, and Wiltshire.

variants.name: Ley
meanings: English: variant of   Lee  .
German: from a medieval personal name, Eloy, from Latin Eligius (see Eligio ), a Christian saint's name.
German (Rhineland): topographic name from Middle High German leie ‘rock, stone, slate’, or a habitational name from any of several places called with this word. Compare Leier .

variants.name: Lees
meanings: English and Scottish: topographic name from Middle English lees ‘fields, arable land’, plural of lee (see Lee ), or from Middle English lese ‘pasture, meadow’ (Old English lǣs).

English: habitational name fromLeece or Lees in Lancashire, or Leese in Cheshire, all named from OldEnglish lēas ‘woodland clearings’ (plural oflēah), or from Leece in Cumbria, which was probably namedwith a Celtic word, lïss ‘hall, court, the principalhouse in a district’.
English: variant of   Leece   1.

variants.name: Lay
meanings: English: variant of   Lee  .
Scottish: shortened form of   McClay  .
German: habitational name from any of the places so named, in the Rhineland near Koblenz and in Bavaria, from lay(h), a word meaning ‘stone, rock, slate’.

variants.name: Laye
meanings: English: variant of   Lee  .
Chinese: variant Romanization of the surname 李, based on its Cantonese pronunciation. See   Li   1.

variants.name: Lie
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Scandinavian Erik, Oystein, Sven, Thor.  German Hans, Gunther, Hilde.  Korean Han, Chang Ho, Chen, Chun, Hok, Jung, Kam, Liang, Lie, Ling, Shuen, Yung.
Norwegian: variant of Li 11, a habitational name from any of numerous farmsteads named Li, from Old Norse hlíth ‘mountain slope, hillside’. In Norway this is the most frequent spelling.
German: unexplained.

variants.name: Layman
meanings: English: topographic name for a dweller by a woodland clearing or a meadow, from Middle English ley + man. Compare Lee .
English: nickname from layman, a word denoting an uneducated person.
Americanized form of German   Lehmann  .

variants.name: Lea
meanings: English: variant of   Lee  .
Chinese: variant Romanization of the surnames 李 and 利, see   Li   1 and 3.

variants.name: Lye
meanings: English: variant of   Lee   1, a topographic name for someone who lived near a patch of arable land, or a habitational name from Lye in Herefordshire (with the same etymology).

Norwegian: habitational name from a farmstead in Rogaland named Lye, Old Norse Lýgi meaning ‘alliance, covenant’, used to denote a place sanctified by such an agreement, such as a court or council meeting place.
Altered form of German   Ley  .

variants.name: Saelee
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Southeast Asian Chan, Kao, Nai, Chai, Cheng, Chien, Foo, Hin, Muang, Pao, Seng, Song, Sou.
Southeast Asian (Iu Mien): from the name of the Lee clan of the Iu Mien people from Laos + the Thai prefix sae, denoting a clan. Compare Lee 8.

variants.name: Leigh
meanings: English: habitational name from any of numerous places (in at least sixteen counties, but especially Leigh in Lancashire) named either with the nominative case of Old English lēah ‘woodland clearing’ (see Lee ) or with lēage, a late dative form of this word (see Lye ).
Chinese: variant Romanization of the surname 李, based on its Cantonese pronunciation. See   Li   1.

nameMeanings.name: Castle
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: topographic name from Anglo-Norman French, Middle English castel ‘castle, fortified building or set of buildings’, especially the residence of a feudal lord (from Late Latin castellum, a diminutive of castrum ‘fort, Roman walled city’). The name would also have denoted a servant who lived and worked at such a place, or someone who had an obligation to provide services or rent to a castle.

variants.name: Castell
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Roberto, Amarilys, Carlos, Eduardo, Estevan, Francisco, Guadalupe, Humberto, Jorge, Manuel, Mateo, Pablo.
English and Scottish: variant of   Castle  .

Manx: shortened form of Gaelic Mac Asgaill ‘son of Ásketill’, an Old Norse personal name composed of the elements áss ‘god’ + ketill ‘sacrificial cauldron, helmet’. The Manx surname is cognate with Scottish Gaelic McAskill .

variants.name: Cassell
meanings: English and Irish: habitational name of Norman origin, from Cassel in the Nord department, Flanders, France.
English: sometimes a variant of   Castle  .
Irish: shortened Anglicized form of Ó Caisile, which is probably in origin a variant of Ó Caiside, see Cassidy .

variants.name: Castel
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Benoit, Herve, Jean Michel, Thierry, Yves.  Spanish Jose, Blanco, Carlos, Rafael.
English: variant of   Castle  .
Aragonese, Catalan, and Jewish (Sephardic): variant of   Castell   3, a cognate of 5 below.

variants.name: Castles
meanings: English: variant of Castle , with post-medieval excrescent -s.
Irish (Armagh and Meath): in Ireland, this may be the English name, but it may also be an Anglicized form of Ó Caisil; see Cashell .

nameMeanings.name: Sir
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From Old Norse, originally a byname meaning ‘bride, wife’; in mythology the name of Thor's golden-haired wife.

nameMeanings.name: Robert
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the many French names of Germanic origin that were introduced into Britain by the Normans; it has since remained in continuous use. It is derived from the nearly synonymous elements hrōd ‘fame’ + berht ‘bright, famous’, and had a native Old English predecessor of similar form (Hreodbeorht), which was supplanted by the Norman name. Two dukes of Normandy in the 11th century bore the name: the father of William the Conqueror (sometimes identified with the legendary Robert the Devil), and his eldest son. It was borne also by three kings of Scotland, notably Robert the Bruce ( 1274–1329 ), who freed Scotland from English domination. The altered short form Bob is very common, but Hob and Dob, which were common in the Middle Ages and gave rise to surnames, are extinct. See also Rupert .

en.lifeSummary: When Sir Robert Lockhart, Baron of Lee Castle was born in 1591, in Cleghorn, Lanarkshire, Scotland, United Kingdom, his father, Lord, Alan Lockhart VI of Ceghorn, was 39 and his mother, Lady, Elizabeth, M. Lockhart of Cleghorn, was 31. He married Dame, Anna Elphinstone in 1617, in Scotland, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 1 daughter. He died on 2 March 1663, in his hometown, at the age of 72.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir Robert Lockhart, Baron of Lee Castle est né en 1591, à Cleghorn, Lanarkshire, Écosse, Royaume-Uni, son père, Lord, Alan Lockhart VI of Ceghorn, avait 39 ans et sa mère, Lady, Elizabeth, M. Lockhart of Cleghorn, avait 31 ans. Il a épousé Dame, Anna Elphinstone en 1617, à Écosse, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 1 fille. Il est décédé le 2 mars 1663, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 72 ans.

Morì il 2 marzo 1663, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 72 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir Robert Lockhart, Baron of Lee Castleが1591年にイギリススコットランドラナルクシュアイアクレグホールンで生まれた時，父Lord, Alan Lockhart VI of Ceghornは39歳，母Lady, Elizabeth, M. Lockhart of Cleghornは31歳であった。1617年にイギリススコットランドでDame, Anna Elphinstoneと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼は1663年3月2日に故郷で72歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir Robert Lockhart, Baron of Lee Castle(이)가 1591년 영국 스코틀랜드 랜아르크실르 클레고른에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Lord, Alan Lockhart VI of Ceghorn(은)는 39세였고, 그의 어머니 Lady, Elizabeth, M. Lockhart of Cleghorn(은)는 31세였다. 그는 1617년 영국 스코틀랜드에서 Dame, Anna Elphinstone(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1663년 3월 2일 그의 고향에서 72세의 나이로 사망했다.

Ele faleceu em 2 de março de 1663, em sua cidade natal, com 72 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir Robert Lockhart, Baron of Lee Castle родился в 1591 г. в местности клегаурн, ланаркшир, Шотландия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени Lord, Alan Lockhart VI of Ceghorn было 39 лет, а матери, которую звали Lady, Elizabeth, M. Lockhart of Cleghorn, 31. Он женился на женщине по имени Dame, Anna Elphinstone в 1617 г. в местности Шотландия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 1 дочь. Он умер 2 марта 1663 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 72 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Sir Robert Lockhart, Baron of Lee Castle nació en 1591, en Cleghorn, Lanarkshire, Escocia, Reino Unido, su papá, Lord, Alan Lockhart VI of Ceghorn, tenía 39 años y su mamá, Lady, Elizabeth, M. Lockhart of Cleghorn, tenía 31 años. Se casó con Dame, Anna Elphinstone en 1617, en Escocia, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijos y 1 hija. Murió el 2 de marzo de 1663, en su pueblo, a la edad de 72 años.
